hypothesis: Replacing the single hardwired `com.ovp2.daily` OS unit with a job registry (.ovp/schedule.json) dispatched by one OS entry running `ovp2 schedule tick` every 10 min lets the pipeline heartbeat run daily AND weekly crystallize without a human, and lets a future desktop app reuse the same engine (portable registry + ovp-scheduler crate). Predicted: daily still runs at its configured local time; crystallize (previously never automated — daily builds reader packs but never crystallizes) runs once weekly; a freshly installed job first fires at its NEXT occurrence, not immediately; a due-job failure surfaces via the tick's nonzero exit; concurrent tick/run-now and every crystal writer serialize on the vault locks, so no scheduled run corrupts shared state.

predicted_delta.daily_dispatch: unchanged local-time cadence, now via the registry tick
predicted_delta.fresh_job_first_run: next occurrence, never immediately on install
predicted_delta.failed_job_visibility: tick exits nonzero; `schedule status` shows the failed job

guardrails.no_new_persistent_state_layer: schedule-state.json is a rebuildable projection of run history, not a ledger
guardrails.single_writer_lock: crystal-synth/-write/-review-apply all take .ovp/run.lock; scheduler dispatch serializes on .ovp/scheduler.lock

eval_plan.replay_set: unit tests in crates/ovp-scheduler/src/lib.rs (cadence/is_due/plan_tick/persistence) + crates/ovp-cli/src/commands/schedule.rs (install/uninstall/migration/rollback via a modeled OS runner) + scheduler.rs adapter (env resolution, seed-state, missing-registry). 60+ tests, no live OS scheduler required.
eval_plan.live_evidence: manual `ovp2 schedule tick` smoke on a temp vault: due jobs run + state recorded incrementally, disabled jobs skipped, run-now forces, fail-exit non-zero.

rollback: Revert to a single `ovp2 schedule install` daily unit: the OS unit is regenerated by `install`, and `schedule.json`/`schedule-state.json` are disposable projections (no ledger surgery). Removing runtime.scheduler from evolution/components.json and deleting the two projection files returns the vault to a no-scheduler state; `ovp2 daily`/`crystal-synth` remain runnable by hand exactly as before.
